axis_responsibility.Agent: This return is design-lead support evidence only and does not classify success, quality, or route.
axis_responsibility.Brick: The task and Building Plan declaration define the work and graph edges; declared terminal graph edges are Brick work composition evidence, not closure execution.
axis_responsibility.Evidence: raw/link.jsonl rows are observed evidence; raw:link-graph:* rows are declaration records, while raw:link:* closed-boundary rows are executed Link evidence for this predicate.
axis_responsibility.Link: Closed-boundary Movement meaning remains Link-owned; the observer may read recorded Link evidence but must not choose Movement, target, route, or sufficiency.
axis_responsibility.Support: frontier_observation, ledger/dashboard projection, and checker fixtures are support mechanics that read evidence and expose the bug/regression pin.

Positive pin: fixture with raw:link:* closed-boundary row and building_lifecycle_state closed must observe complete/closed projection.

returned.design_summary: Repair should make closure observation depend on executed Link evidence, not declared graph plan-edge evidence. The low-blast-radius design is to extract one private predicate, for example _is_executed_closed_boundary_record(record), and use it from both _closed_boundary_raw_record_after_latest_pause and _closed_boundary_observed. The predicate should require raw_ref.startswith('raw:link:') plus _is_closed_boundary_ref(target). This mirrors the already-correct sibling helper and excludes raw:link-graph:* and raw:link-frontier:* without relying on optional boolean annotations. The checker fixture should also keep an executed closure row carrying building_lifecycle_state == 'closed' so lifecycle remains observed evidence, even if raw_ref namespace is the predicate gate.
edge_cases: A declared graph row missing declared_graph_edge but still using raw:link-graph:* must remain non-closing.
A record with raw_ref raw:link-frontier:* and closed-looking text must remain non-closing.
A non-boundary target that merely ends with closed must remain non-closing through _is_closed_boundary_ref.
A paused/resumed path must use the same executed-record predicate after latest pause, preventing helper drift.
A building with agent_received_records greater than agent_return_records must continue to report agent_incomplete before the fallback, even if declared terminal graph rows exist.
Synthetic checker fixtures should include building_lifecycle_state closed on executed closure rows to keep fixture shape close to real ledger evidence.
invariants: A declared graph terminal edge alone must never produce frontier_kind complete.
An executed raw:link:* record targeting a closed building boundary must still produce complete.
raw:link-frontier:* records must not be accepted as executed closure records.
declared_graph_edge truthy rows must be ignored for closure even if their target is a closed boundary.
The fallback must not inspect process liveness or infer provider state.
Checker green, dashboard rendering, and projection freshness remain support evidence only.

Use raw_ref namespace as the structural discriminator: include raw:link: executed rows; exclude raw:link-graph:* declared graph rows and raw:link-frontier:* observation rows; then require _is_closed_boundary_ref(target).
Keep branch order above the fallback byte-identical; do not move chat_session_parked, agent_incomplete, link_paused, human_review_waiting, or pause-after-close branches.

Add a mutation probe that replaces the fixed predicate with the current broken behavior, proving the declared graph terminal fixture turns closed and the checker fires RED.
